title: Hand on heart, naming what is there
subtitle: A somatic bedtime meditation for placing a hand on the chest and naming the emotional state without analysis.
excerpt: A short somatic meditation for Day 6 of the SLEEP program. Hand on heart, one slow breath, and a plain name for what is present. Not a solution practice, a settling one.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 6 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The hand-on-heart gesture draws on self-compassion practices in the tradition of Kristin Neff.

content.text: A short somatic meditation for Day 6 of the SLEEP program. The practice uses one gesture (hand on heart) and one task: name what is actually there, without analysis, without trying to change it.
Lie or sit comfortably. Place your dominant hand flat on your chest, over your heart.
Feel the warmth of your hand through your clothing. Feel the slight weight of it.
Take one slow breath in. Let it out slowly.
Say, in your mind or aloud: What is here right now?
Notice what comes. Let yourself name it simply, tired, worried, relieved, flat, tender, heavy. No explanation required.
Say: I see it. It is here. It is allowed to be here.
Take two more slow breaths with your hand still on your chest. Feel whether the pressure in your chest shifts at all, not because you are making it shift, but because it has been seen.

Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 6 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The hand-on-heart gesture draws on self-compassion practices in the tradition of Kristin Neff.
